q1.question: What is FreeSocks?
q1.answer: A free VPN service that helps people in heavily-censored regions reach the open internet. It's operated by Unredacted, a US 501(c)(3) nonprofit.

q2.question: Is it really free?
q2.answer: Yes. A free account gives you a working VPN. A paid FreeSocks membership lifts the limits and helps fund free access for others.

q3.question: Do I need to give an email or password?
q3.answer: No. You pass a one-time human check and we generate a 32-digit account number - that's your only credential. We never ask for an email, phone number, or name.

q4.question: What if I lose my account number?
q4.answer: It's the only way back into your account and we can't recover it (we store only a hashed version), so save it in a password manager when you create it. If you lose it, just create a new free account.

q5.question: How do I connect?
q5.answer: Create a subscription, copy its link (or scan the QR code), and add it to a compatible app. Your account page lists the recommended app for each platform (Android, iPhone, Windows, macOS / Linux).

q6.question: What do you log about me?
q6.answer: As little as possible: only a hashed version of your account number - never your email, name, or IP address - and no logs of the sites you visit or the traffic you send.

q7.question: The link is blocked where I am. What can I do?
q7.answer: On your account page, open "Trouble connecting?" to get a mirror link served from a different host that may not be blocked. You can also set your delivery preference to favor staying connected.

q8.question: Can I buy a membership for someone else?
q8.answer: Yes - on your account page use "Buy codes to share" to purchase membership codes you can give to friends or family. Each one works on any account and doesn't affect yours.

q9.question: Can I pay anonymously?
q9.answer: Yes. You can pay with cryptocurrency - Bitcoin, or Monero and Zcash for the most privacy - with no account, email, or card. Your membership activates automatically once the payment confirms.

q10.question: Do you own your servers, or rent them?
q10.answer: We own our hardware and run it ourselves. Plenty of VPN companies rent theirs from datacenters or cloud hosts, which puts the machine your traffic passes through in someone else's hands - they can be pressured to hand it over, or just quietly copy what is on it. That is a line we are not willing to cross, so we buy and run our own equipment instead. It costs more and we grow slower because of it, but it is the only way the privacy promises on this page actually mean anything.

threat.subtitle: An honest look at what this service can and cannot do. Security tools that overpromise get people hurt, so here is exactly where the lines are.

q1.question: What does FreeSocks protect me from?
q1.answer: FreeSocks tunnels your traffic through an encrypted VPN connection, so your ISP, mobile carrier, school, or workplace network cannot see which sites you visit or block them. It is built for getting past censorship and for keeping the network you are on from watching what you do.

q2.question: What does FreeSocks NOT protect me from?
q2.answer: It does not make you anonymous to sites you sign in to: if you log in to an account, that site knows who you are. It cannot protect a device that is already compromised (spyware, a managed work profile, someone with physical access). And a powerful adversary that can watch traffic at many points on the internet may still correlate patterns. If your safety depends on strong anonymity, use Tor and follow specialist guidance for your situation.

q3.question: Can FreeSocks see my traffic?
q3.answer: Your traffic exits through our servers, so treat us like any exit: sites you use over HTTPS (almost all of the modern web) stay encrypted end to end and we cannot read their contents. We configure our servers to keep no connection logs, no visited-site logs, and no source IPs, and the control plane never stores your IP address at all.

q4.question: What happens if a FreeSocks server is seized or compromised?
q4.answer: There is nothing identifying on it. Servers hold no names, emails, IPs, or traffic history, because we never collect those in the first place. Access keys can be revoked and reissued quickly, and we can rotate infrastructure without losing accounts.

q5.question: Can my government or ISP tell that I am using FreeSocks?
q5.answer: Sometimes censors can detect that circumvention traffic is in use even when they cannot read it. Our transports are designed to look like ordinary encrypted web traffic, and Internet Freedom Mode routes through infrastructure that is expensive to block. Still, whether use is detectable, and what the consequences are, varies by country. Weigh your local risk.

q6.question: Why should I believe any of this?
q6.answer: The control plane is open source, so anyone can read exactly what it stores and check that there is no place a name, email, or IP address could even go. Claims that depend on server configuration (like disabled logging) are documented and enforced by the same code. You still have to trust the operator for the parts you cannot see, as with every VPN; our approach is to minimize what there is to trust.

q7.question: Do payments link my identity to my browsing?
q7.answer: Membership is optional, and payment is handled by outside processors: we never store the payer's name, email, or address, and an order is tied to your account only through an opaque reference. Cryptocurrency options (including Monero) exist for people who do not want a card trail at all.

common.copyFailed: Copy failed - select the text and copy it manually

captcha.failedBody: The check runs on your device and didn't finish. This is usually a network problem, not something you did wrong.
captcha.failedTip1: Wait a moment, then try again
captcha.failedTip2: Try a different network - or a VPN if sites are blocked where you are
captcha.failedTip3: Still stuck? Try a private/incognito window or turn off browser extensions

reveal.title: Save your account number now
reveal.subtitle: This 32-digit number is the ONLY way to sign in again. There is no email or password to recover it. If you lose it, your account is gone for good.
reveal.cannotRecover: We cannot recover it for you - not even support can.
reveal.saveHint: Save it in a password manager, or write it down somewhere safe and private.
reveal.downloadRequired: Download your account number to continue. Keep the file somewhere safe.
reveal.verifySubtitle: Your account number is now hidden. Enter or paste it from the copy you just saved to confirm you can sign in later.
reveal.verifyMismatch: That doesn't match your account number. Check the copy you saved, or go back to see it again.
reveal.leaveWarning: Your account number is still on screen. If you leave now without saving it, you will not be able to sign in again.

support.label: Support ID
support.hint: Share this if you contact us. It is NOT your sign-in number and grants no access.
support.getAccountLine: Questions or problems? Email us at

login.title: Sign in with your account number
login.subtitle: Enter the 32-digit account number you saved. It's the only way to sign in - there's no email or password to recover.
login.label: Account number
login.noAccount: Don't have an account number yet?
login.sessionExpired: Please sign in again - your session may have ended.
login.popBroken: Your browser can't secure this session because Web Workers are blocked or unavailable. Enable them (check your browser's privacy settings) or try a different browser, then sign in again.

passkey.title: Passkeys
passkey.desc: Sign in with Face ID, Touch ID, a security key, or your password manager - no account number to type.
passkey.warning: Heads-up: a passkey saved on your phone or in your browser may sync to your Apple or Google account, which can link this anonymous account to that identity. Use a hardware security key, or skip passkeys, if that matters to you.
passkey.unsupported: This device or browser doesn't support passkeys.
passkey.none: No passkeys yet. Your account number still signs you in.

account.title: Your account
account.redeemSuccess: Redeemed - you're now on {tier} for {days} more days.
account.redeemFailed: That code is not valid, or has already been used.
account.noSubBody: Create your key to get a link you can use in any compatible VPN app.
account.rotateBody: A new 32-digit number is generated and shown once. Your current number stops working immediately. Anyone who has it loses access. Do this if your number may have leaked.
account.rotateFailedTitle: Could not change the account number
account.refreshMembership: Already paid? Check for my membership
account.regenSuccessBody: Re-import it on each of your devices. The old URL works for 24 more hours.
account.regenFailedTitle: Could not create a new key
account.switchSuccessBodyGrace: Re-import the new subscription URL on each device. The old subscription works for 24 more hours.
account.switchSuccessBody: Re-import the new subscription URL on each device.
account.refreshNoneBody: If you just paid, give it a moment and try again.
account.graceTitle: Your account is in a grace period
account.graceBody: Your membership has lapsed, so this account will be limited soon. Renew - donate or redeem a membership code below - to keep your plan.
account.disabledBody: New keys and changes are paused on this account. Redeem a membership code below to reactivate it, or contact support and share your Support ID.
account.rotateHint: Replace your 32-digit account number if it may have leaked - or, if you never saved it, rotate now to get a fresh one you can save. The old one stops working immediately.
account.keyActionsHint: These change your VPN connection only - your 32-digit account number stays the same.

account.deviceRevokedBody: The slot is free. That device loses access until it re-imports your subscription.

hero.importOpenHint: Tap to import on this device, or scan the code from your phone.
hero.scanFallback: Scan the fallback on another device
hero.fallbackHint: Use this if the main link gets blocked
hero.configBelowNote: Your full configuration is below - add the servers by hand. For privacy, the auto-updating subscription link isn't shown by default (your app would fetch it through a CDN).
hero.urlDangerBody: This link works in any app, but the app then downloads your configuration through a third-party CDN in plain text - the CDN operator can see your server details and that you use FreeSocks. That is exactly what this focus avoids. Use it only if your app cannot import the configuration below.
hero.nearlyOut: Nearly out, only {amount} left this period.
hero.nodeOnlineHint: The server behind your config is up and responding. If you still can't connect, your network or ISP is likely filtering it - try another connection mode or location.
hero.nodeOfflineBody: The server behind your config is currently offline. This is on our side, not your network. Try again in a few minutes, or create a new config (optionally in a different location).
hero.keyLimited: You've used all your data for this period. It resets automatically, or you can upgrade for more.
hero.keyExpired: This key has expired. Renew your membership or create a new key to reconnect.
hero.keyDisabled: This key is currently disabled. If your membership lapsed, renew it; otherwise contact support with your support ID.

location.pickerHint: Where your config's server is. Automatic picks the least busy location; pick one yourself if it works better on your network.

regen.title: Create a new subscription URL?

regen.point1: Your current key remains usable for the next 24 hours
regen.point2: You'll need to re-import the new URL in each of your devices

switch.point1: A new subscription URL is issued on the {to} backend
switch.point2: The current {from} URL keeps working for 24 hours, then is deleted
switch.point3: You'll need to re-import the new URL in each VPN client you use

match.countPlural=one: You currently have 1 connected device - re-import on it
match.countPlural=other: You currently have {count} connected devices - re-import on all of them

get.freeAccountNoteNoDevices: Free accounts are valid for {days} days. No email or password.

get.subErrorSafePrefix: Your account is safe. You can create your key later from

get.plansSubtitle: The free plan is yours already, and your key works either way. Membership is an optional upgrade that supports the network.

impact.title: Donations support Unredacted
impact.body: Unredacted is a US 501(c)(3) nonprofit. FreeSocks is one of the projects it runs. Donations fund the work. See what that work is on the Unredacted site.
impact.collectiveBody: Donations made through FreeSocks raise every free user's monthly bandwidth for the month they're given. This is what the community's donations are doing right now.
impact.bonusThisMonthDetail: on top of every free account's monthly allowance
impact.usersHelpedDetail: active free users whose allowance the bonus raises

impact.empty: No donations yet this month - the first one starts the counter.
impact.externalNote: This counter tracks donations made through FreeSocks only. Gifts made directly at unredacted.org/donate support Unredacted's wider work, but don't add bandwidth here.

donate.addSubtitle: Chip in to help keep FreeSocks free for everyone.
donate.standaloneSubtitle: FreeSocks is free for everyone, funded by donations. Give any amount to help keep it running - a donation also raises this month's bandwidth for every free user.
donate.impact: Adds about {gb} GB to every free user this month
donate.bonusActive: Donations this month have added {gb} GB to every free user's monthly allowance.
donate.thanksBody: Thank you - your support helps keep FreeSocks free for everyone.

referral.fieldHint: Have a code from a friend? You each get bonus membership days when you upgrade.
referral.applied: Referral applied — bonus days are yours when you upgrade.
referral.cardBody: Share your link. When someone signs up with it and becomes a member, you each get bonus days — theirs right away, yours after {vestingDays} days.

qr.ariaLabel: QR code for the subscription URL

app.adminLoadFailedBody: The network request for this section failed. Reload to retry.

renew.body: FreeSocks is community-funded - donations keep it running. To renew your membership, donate or contact us for a membership code.
renew.lapsedBody: You're on the free tier now. Renew below to restore your membership.

upgrade.title: Upgrade to a FreeSocks membership
upgrade.extendStacks: Time you add stacks on top of your current end date.
upgrade.subtitle: Unlimited bandwidth and devices. Choose a length and how to pay.
upgrade.subtitleNoDevices: Unlimited bandwidth. Choose a length and how to pay.
upgrade.cryptoMinNote: Crypto payments start at {months} months - shorter terms fall below the network minimum. Pick another method for shorter terms.
upgrade.cryptoPrivacyNote: No account, email, or card needed - pay privately. Monero and Zcash offer the most privacy.
upgrade.noStoreNote: We never see your card or wallet, and store no payment details.
upgrade.confirmingBody: Crypto can take a few minutes to confirm. You can leave this page - your membership activates automatically.
upgrade.paidBody: Thank you! Your membership is now active.
upgrade.failedBody: Your payment did not go through, or the checkout expired. You can try again.

gift.title: Buy codes to share
gift.subtitle: Purchase membership codes to give to friends or family. Each works on any account and never touches yours.
gift.boughtEmpty: You haven't bought any codes yet.

reveal.leaveWarning: Your codes are still on screen. If you leave now without saving them, you will not be able to see them again.

error.offline: You appear to be offline. Check your connection and try again.
error.rateLimited: Too many attempts. Please wait a minute and try again.
error.backendUnavailable: No VPN server is available right now. Your account is safe - try creating your key again in a few minutes.
error.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
error.captchaFailed: The human check failed. Please complete it and try again.
error.captchaUnconfigured: The service is temporarily unavailable. Please try again in a few minutes.
error.renderBody: The page failed to render. This is a bug in the app, and refreshing usually fixes it. If it keeps happening, please report it.
error.sessionExpired: Your session has ended. Please sign in again.
error.invalidAccountId: That account number wasn't recognized. Check it and try again.
error.codeInvalid: That code can't be redeemed. Check it for typos and try again.
error.changeInProgress: Another change is already in progress - try again in a moment.
error.noPeerTier: Switching isn't available for your plan yet.
error.deviceNotFound: That device is no longer on your account.
error.deviceUnsupported: Your current server type doesn't support removing individual devices.
error.billing: The payment service couldn't process this. Please try again later.
error.serverError: The server had a problem handling this. Please try again in a few minutes.
error.modeUnavailable: Your current connection mode is no longer available. Switch to another mode first, then try again.

setup.title: Set up your VPN app
setup.noApps: No recommended apps for this platform yet - use any compatible client and add your subscription manually.
setup.intro: Copy your subscription link above, then add it to a compatible app:

step.import: Open it, add a subscription / profile, and paste your link
step.importConfig: Open it, choose to add servers manually, and enter the configuration shown below

setup.hwidNote: On a device-limited plan, turn on "device identification" (HWID) in the app's settings so your device is recognized.
setup.deviceIncompatibleNote: These apps don't identify your device, so each launch can use up a device slot - or fail to connect on a device-limited plan. Prefer an app above.

clientDesc.hiddify: The easiest all-round choice: one-tap import, a clean interface, and builds for every platform.
clientDesc.karing: Feature-rich and honors your plan's device limit. Slightly busier than Hiddify, but works well everywhere.
clientDesc.anywhere: A polished, easy client for Apple devices only: iPhone, iPad, Apple TV, and Mac. No Android, Windows, or Linux version.
clientDesc.singBox: The reference app for the sing-box core. Powerful but minimal, so it's best if you are comfortable with technical settings.
clientDesc.v2rayng: A long-standing, lightweight Android client with one-tap import. More utilitarian than Hiddify, but dependable.
clientDesc.v2rayn: A powerful desktop app for advanced users. Import is manual, and links using encryption "none" can need a manual tweak. Prefer Hiddify if that sounds fiddly.
clientDesc.clash: Clash Verge, a popular desktop app with strong routing rules. Some versions reject VLESS subscription links; if yours won't import, use Hiddify or v2rayN instead.
clientDesc.flclash: A clean, cross-platform Clash-family app. Import by pasting your subscription link (no one-tap import).
clientDesc.mihomoParty: A friendly desktop app for the Clash (mihomo) core. Paste your subscription link to import.
clientDesc.throne: An advanced desktop client that honors your plan's device limit, with solid Linux support. Expect manual setup.
clientDesc.shadowrocket: A paid, closed-source iOS app that is popular and reliable. Worth using if you already own it; the open-source apps above do the same job for free.
clientDesc.outline: The simplest experience there is: paste your access key and connect. Only works with Outline (Shadowsocks) access keys.

mirror.explainer: If your normal subscription link won't connect where you are, add a mirror link below. It serves the same key from a different host that may not be blocked.
mirror.unavailable: Mirrors aren't available on this deployment right now - there are no mirror links to add.
mirror.addToAppHint: Add each one as an extra subscription in your app, then try connecting.
mirror.regionNotStored: Used only to pick a nearby mirror - it isn't stored.
mirror.capped: You've added the maximum number of mirrors.
mirror.exhausted: No more mirrors are available for your region right now.
mirror.noSubscription: Create your key first, then you can add a mirror.

rawconfig.title: Your configuration
rawconfig.explainer: Your full VPN configuration, fetched over an encrypted channel so it never crosses a CDN in plain text. Copy it into your app by hand instead of using a subscription link.
rawconfig.addHint: Paste these server entries into your VPN app manually.

delivery.title: What matters most to you?
delivery.subtitle: Pick a focus. It's saved on this device only, and you can change it anytime.
delivery.subtitleServer: Pick a focus. Changing it moves your existing key to the matching servers - your subscription URL stays the same.
delivery.subtitleSignup: Pick a focus. It's saved to your account, and your first key uses it - you can change it anytime.
delivery.evadeBody: Pick this if websites, apps, or VPNs are blocked where you are. Built to keep working under censorship, with backup links that are harder to block.
delivery.privacyBody: Pick this if the internet is mostly open where you are. The strongest confidentiality - your configuration stays off third-party servers - but it is easier for censors to block.
delivery.confirmBody: This moves your existing key to the {label} servers, keeping the same subscription URL. Your apps keep working and pick up the new servers on their next refresh.
delivery.confirmPoint1: Your key moves to the {label} servers - same subscription URL, nothing to re-import
delivery.confirmPoint2: Takes effect within a minute - reconnect in your app if it doesn't refresh on its own
delivery.confirmPoint3: Using the raw config? Copy the new one after switching

match.countPlural=one: Your 1 connected device will reconnect to the new servers
match.countPlural=other: Your {count} connected devices will reconnect to the new servers

delivery.switchSuccessBodyGrace: Re-import the new subscription URL on each device. Your old key works for 24 more hours.
delivery.switchSuccessBody: Same subscription URL - your apps will pick up the new servers on their next refresh.

globe.title: Voices from behind the firewall
globe.body: Every day, people behind national firewalls use FreeSocks to read, share, and publish - and to be heard.
globe.aria: A rotating globe: labels with voices from censored countries

ir.l1: They filter the internet; they can't filter the truth.
ir.l2: My report on the protests reached the world.
ir.l3: My students read what the state calls lies.

cn.l1: History should not be a banned word.
cn.l2: I shared what happened in my city - and it stayed up.
cn.l3: I archived the deleted posts before they vanished.

ru.l1: The truth should not need a permit.
ru.l2: Independent journalism is not a crime.
ru.l3: A blocked newspaper still gets read.

tm.l1: A whole country, almost offline - and still heard.
tm.l2: They control the media, not my voice.
tm.l3: Silence is the law here; we whisper anyway.

cu.l1: My voice travels farther than I ever will.
cu.l2: We document what the state denies.
cu.l3: Independent voices, hand to hand, screen to screen.

by.l1: When they shut us down, we still spoke.
by.l3: They banned our flag; not our voice.

mm.l1: The blackout did not silence us.
mm.l2: Evidence of the crackdown got out.
mm.l3: When the towers fell silent, the story did not.

ve.l1: We count the votes they won't.
ve.l2: Hunger is not a state secret.
ve.l3: The queue for food is long; the truth is longer.

vn.l1: Writing about corruption is not a crime.
vn.l3: One article they deleted reached thousands.

pk.l1: During the shutdown, witnesses still spoke.
pk.l2: Silencing journalists won't hide the story.
pk.l3: The channel went dark; the reporting did not.

eg.l1: They jailed the bloggers, not the words.
eg.l3: From a small screen, a big story.

sa.l2: Her voice reached beyond the wall.
sa.l3: She asked a question the kingdom bans.

et.l2: Witnesses still found a way out.
et.l3: The dead were counted, despite the blackout.

tr.l1: Blocking the site won't block the story.
tr.l2: Journalism continues, court order or not.
tr.l3: An arrested anchor cannot sign off the news.

az.l3: They froze our accounts, not our work.

uz.l1: A closed internet is not a quiet one.
uz.l2: The world still heard what happened here.
uz.l3: The squares are watched; the words still move.

impact.title: Donations at work
impact.body: Every donation made through FreeSocks raises the monthly bandwidth of every free account for that month. This is what donors have added so far - you could add to it too.
impact.chartAria: Bandwidth added to every free user by donations, month by month

hero.subtitle: FreeSocks is made for people whose internet is censored, and works as a privacy-respecting VPN anywhere else. Signing up takes one quick human check. We never ask for an email or a password. Your subscription URL works in most modern VPN apps, and a membership gets you {limits}.
hero.impactNote: Donations made through FreeSocks directly power free users: every donation buys real bandwidth for people in censored countries, that same month.

freeCard.title: Free tier
freeCard.urlBody: Xray-powered VLESS. Paste into any compatible client.
freeCard.noAuthBody: One human-check. Save your account number to sign in. No email collected.
freeCard.footnote: Numbers reflect the current free-tier configuration. Solve the check to get yours.
freeCard.upsellBody: Get {limits} - and help keep FreeSocks free for others.
freeCard.cryptoNote: Crypto accepted - Bitcoin, Monero, Zcash and more

noAuth.title: No email or password
noAuth.body: One human-check and you are in. We mint a 32-digit account number you save to sign back in. No email collected.

mirrors.title: Mirror URLs
mirrors.body: Subscriptions are mirrored across multiple providers so a single block does not cut you off.

protocols.title: Standard protocols
protocols.body: Xray-powered VLESS. Works in most VPN clients.

privacy.title: What we store
privacy.subtitle: FreeSocks is built to know as little about you as possible.
privacy.point1: We store only a hashed version of your account number - never the number itself.
privacy.point2: No email, phone number, or name. We never ask for them.
privacy.point3: No logs of the sites you visit or the traffic you send - and we don't store your IP address, on our servers or the VPN nodes.
privacy.point4: We store no payment details - you pay on the provider's own page, and the provider never sees your account or VPN subscription.

s1.title: Create a free account
s1.body: Solve a quick human-check. You get a 32-digit account number to save: it is how you sign back in.

s2.title: Create your subscription
s2.body: Once you are signed in, create a subscription URL, with a QR code for handoff to a phone.

s3.title: Paste it into a VPN client
s3.body: Add the URL as a subscription in any compatible client.

membership.title: Membership
membership.descriptionFallback: A FreeSocks membership lifts every limit.
membership.payNote: Pay privately with Bitcoin, Monero, or Zcash - or use a card or PayPal.

about.title: About FreeSocks
about.body2: Most VPNs assume you can pay for a subscription and safely hand over an email address. In much of the world neither is true, so FreeSocks asks for neither. Anyone can get a working key in about a minute and keep it for as long as they use it.
about.openSource: The code that runs this service is published for anyone to inspect, audit, or run themselves.
about.fact3Body: Free accounts are paid for by donations and memberships. There are no ads and nothing is sold.

e2ee.badgeActiveTitle: Encrypted to this server with HPKE. Click to verify.
e2ee.badgeWarnTitle: Couldn't verify the encryption key. Click to verify out-of-band before entering your account number.
e2ee.badgeActiveTitleAdmin: Sensitive member and admin actions are HPKE-encrypted on this deployment. Click for details.
e2ee.badgeWarnTitleAdmin: Couldn't verify this deployment's encryption key. Click for details and out-of-band verification.
e2ee.badgeOffTitle: Standard TLS only. Extra HPKE body encryption isn't enabled on this deployment.
e2ee.bannerWarnDetail: Don't enter your account number yet - verify this connection out-of-band first.
e2ee.verifyIntro: FreeSocks seals your account number and VPN key to this server with HPKE, so a compromised CDN can't read them. These fingerprints identify the keys your browser is using - compare them against the values published out-of-band to be sure they haven't been swapped.
e2ee.protectScope: Your account number and key are encrypted to this server with HPKE, so the network and any CDN in front of it can't read them.
e2ee.protectServerReads: FreeSocks itself can read them to set up your account, so this protects you from the network in between, not from the server.
e2ee.protectTunnel: It's separate from your VPN connection, which is encrypted on its own.
e2ee.protectAdmin: On the admin dashboard, sensitive actions - creating API tokens, invites, and membership codes, and uploading backend, billing, or storage credentials - are HPKE-encrypted to this server too. Routine reads and settings use TLS, your passkey, and proof-of-possession.
e2ee.attestationOk: Verified - the server is attesting a valid key signed by the manifest key your app trusts.
e2ee.attestationFail: Could not verify the server's current key - a network problem, or a CDN tampering with the key endpoint. Verify out-of-band before continuing.
e2ee.attestationUnreachable: The live key check is temporarily unavailable. Your connection still uses the verified key built into the app.
e2ee.attestationUnconfigured: Live key checking isn't set up on this build.
e2ee.compareBody: Compare the fingerprints above against the values published through a channel this server doesn't control. They must match.
e2ee.dnsBody: Look the pin up yourself in a terminal, through your own DNS resolver - a path that doesn't run through this site or its CDN. The answer should contain the same fingerprints shown above. (If it returns nothing, the operator may not have published the record yet; use the signed release instead.)
e2ee.dnsCaveat: Independent only if your DNS isn't run by the same company as the CDN; a DNSSEC-validating resolver is best. For full assurance, confirm the same values in the signed release too.
e2ee.verifierExtension: A verifier browser extension that re-checks this build on every visit is planned, but not available yet.
e2ee.verifierExtensionInstall: Install the verifier extension - it re-checks this build against the published one on every visit (the strongest protection against a tampered page).
e2ee.caveat: This in-page check is a convenience. A tampered page could lie about its own status, so the real proof comes from comparing these values somewhere outside this server, such as the DNS lookup above or a published release.

deviceRevoke.title: Revoke this device?
deviceRevoke.body: The device ending …{suffix} will be disconnected and its slot freed. It can reconnect later by re-importing your subscription URL.

status.title: Network status
status.overallMajor: A major outage is in progress
status.matrixBody: How well each connection mode works from specific countries, based on reports we receive. "Partial" means some networks or times of day block it.
status.incidentsNone: No incidents in the last 30 days.
